component.name: Mackes Desktop Environment
component.summary: Unified Wayland desktop with mesh-first file sharing, notifications, clipboard, and fleet config

description.p: Mackes Desktop Environment (MDE) is a Wayland-only Linux desktop built around a single unified Rust meta-daemon (mded). Every machine in your mesh runs the same daemon; settings, clipboard, notifications, and file shares move between them as a single fabric.

ul.li: Sway compositor + Iced + libcosmic panel with foreign-toplevel-management dock

mded — supervises every long-running task (clipboard, mDNS, mesh-fs, media-sync, remmina-sync, ansible-pull, KDE Connect bridge, heartbeat, notifications, notification relay) as in-process workers under one PID
org.freedesktop.Notifications served by mded; cross-peer notification relay reads ~/QNM-Shared/<peer>/.qnm-notifications/
Settings layer: 29 dot-notated keys covering theme/font/display/power/notification/automount/wallpaper/keybinds/autostart, served over dev.mackes.MDE.Settings
Fleet config: push a setting value to every healthy peer with one CLI call; rollback via the Workbench Revisions panel
MDE Files (Iced + libcosmic, cosmic-files vendored) with Send-To pipeline for cross-peer transfers

description.p: Upgrades from v1.x Mackes Shell are automatic: dnf upgrade lands on mde-2.0.0 via Obsoletes/Provides, the mde-migrate-from-1x helper atomically moves ~/.config/mackes-shell/ to ~/.config/mde/, env-var shim reads MDE_* with MACKES_* fallback for one release, and D-Bus aliases keep the v1.x org.mackes.* service names addressable for one release.

Rebrand to Mackes Desktop Environment (MDE). Wayland-only (sway). Unified Rust meta-daemon (mded) replaces every v1.x Python daemon. Native settings layer + fleet config. See CHANGELOG.md for the full list of moving parts.
